content.text: The Maintenance module shall contain tabs: All, Pending Assignment, In Progress, Review, Done/Closed.
the Maintenance module to display workflow tabs
I can navigate between different task stages easily.
the user opens the Maintenance module
the system shall display tabs: All, Pending Assignment, In Progress, Review, Done/Closed.
When the user clicks Review tab, the system shall display only those issues whose status = “Review”.
view only tasks that are in Review status
I can focus specifically on tasks pending verification.
the user clicks the Review tab
the system loads the filtered list
only issues whose status is Review shall be displayed.
The Review list shall show columns: Priority, Observation ID (link), Rack Name, Bay ID, Element, Activity Phase, Observation, Action Performed, completed on, Completed by
all relevant information shown in the Review table
I can understand completed actions and who performed them.
it shall show columns: Priority, Observation ID (link), Rack Name, Bay ID, Element, Activity Phase, Observation, Action Performed, Completed On, Completed By.
The table shall support pagination with a Rows per page selector and page navigation controls.
I can navigate large datasets easily.
the Review list exceeds one screen
the system shall provide Rows per page selector and page navigation controls.

The system shall provide a Filter button in the header. On click, a filter drawer (defined separately) shall open on the right side, allowing users to filter by Severity, Rack, Bay, Element Type, Activity Phase, Observation Type, completed by, Completed on etc.
filter Review tasks using a filter drawer
I can refine results based on specific conditions.
the user clicks the Filter button
it shall provide filters for Severity, Rack, Bay, Element Type, Activity Phase, Observation Type, Completed By, Completed On, etc.
Priority shall be shown as color-coded flags (e.g., red = critical, yellow = medium, green = low).
I can quickly identify the urgency of each task.
priority shall appear as color-coded flags (e.g., red = critical, yellow = medium, green = low).
Observation ID shall be displayed as clickable links (e.g., TSK-0141).
I can open detailed task information quickly.
the user clicks an Observation ID
the system shall open the Task Details screen in a new view.
The Action performed column shall display context buttons such as: Repair, Replace, Under Observation based on task type & status.
the Action Performed column to show context buttons
I can understand what final action was done on that task.
it shall show buttons such as Repair, Replace, Under Observation depending on task data.
Due Date column shall show the date when the task is due.
I can see when each task was originally due.
it shall show the task due date for each row.
Task status shall follow a predefined workflow: Pending Assignment → In Progress → Review → Done.
tasks to follow a predefined workflow
consistency is maintained across the lifecycle.
status must follow: Pending Assignment → In Progress → Review → Done.
System shall display a Group By dropdown with grouping option Action Required, Priority, Location (Rack -> Bay), Observation type and Activity Phase.
a Group By dropdown for Review tasks
I can group tasks based on meaningful criteria.
the Group By dropdown is available
it shall include: Action Required, Priority, Location (Rack → Bay), Observation Type, Activity Phase.
Selected group option shall regroup the task list instantly without page reload.
I can analyze grouped data without delays.
the user selects a Group By option
the task list shall regroup instantly without page reload.
System shall display a Sort By dropdown in the header on the Maintenance screen across all tabs.
a Sort By dropdown available in the header
I can sort tasks regardless of which Maintenance tab I am in.
the user is on any Maintenance tab
the system shall display a Sort By dropdown.
Sorting options include: Priority (High-> Low), Observation Date (Newest -> Oldest), Rack-> Bay order, Action Required(A-Z) and Activity phase
I can reorder the Review task list meaningfully.
the Sort By dropdown is opened
Observation IDs in the grid shall be displayed as clickable links. On click, the system shall open the Task Details screen for the selected observation in a new view
open Task Details by clicking on an Observation ID
I can review complete task information.
Observation IDs are displayed in the grid
the Task Details screen shall open in a new view.
The top of the screen shall display breadcrumb text Maintenance / Task Details to indicate navigation context.
I understand where I am in the system flow.
the Task Details screen is opened
it shall display breadcrumb Maintenance / Task Details.
The Task Details screen shall display three tabs: Observation, Testing, and Timeline
view Observation, Testing, and Timeline tabs
I can review different aspects of the task.
the system shall show three tabs: Observation, Testing, Timeline.

19.title: Tab Switching Does Not Alter Data

content.text: Switching between tabs shall not modify underlying data; it only changes what is displayed.
switching between tabs to not change any stored data
I can safely browse task information.
the user switches between Observation, Testing, and Timeline tabs
no underlying data shall be modified; only the displayed view shall change.
All information in Observation and Testing tabs shall be view-only for the inspector/assignee (no inline edits).
all information in the Observation and Testing tabs to be read-only
I do not accidentally modify inspection or testing data.
the user is viewing the Observation or Testing tab
all fields shall be view-only with no inline editing allowed.
When the Observation tab is active, the system shall display a Location Details section at the top-left.
a Location Details section displayed when the Observation tab is active
I can immediately understand where the issue is located.
the system shall show a Location Details section at the top-left.
Location Details shall show: Rack Name, Bay ID, Element, and Location.
Location Details to show key identifiers
I can confirm the exact location of the observation.
the Location Details section is displayed
it shall show: Rack Name, Bay ID, Element, Location.
The Location value shall be a clickable link (e.g., “Front Side – Bay 06 – Level 1”) that opens the rack layout / plan view for that location.
the Location value to be clickable
I can open the rack layout or plan view for precise location context.
the user clicks the Location link (e.g., Front Side – Bay 06 – Level 1)
the system shall open the rack layout / plan view for that location.
Below Location Details, the system shall show an Observation Summary section.
an Observation Summary section shown below Location Details
I can review all essential observation attributes.
an Observation Summary section shall be displayed below Location Details.
Observation Summary shall display: Observation, Activity Phase, Severity, ID, Inspection Type, Flag, and Notes / Comments.
I can fully understand the issue.
it shall show: Observation, Activity Phase, Severity, ID, Inspection Type, Flag, Notes / Comments.
Severity shall be displayed as a coloured pill/chip (e.g., RED, AMBER, GREEN).
severity to be shown as a colored chip
I can visually identify the criticality of the observation.
it shall appear as a colored pill/chip (RED, AMBER, GREEN).
Flag shall show one or more labels (e.g., “High Urgency”, “Severity Change”) as chips.
flags to display as labeled chips
important indicators like "High Urgency" are visible.
each flag shall appear as a chip/label (e.g., High Urgency, Severity Change).
Notes / Comments shall show the inspector’s written description of the issue.
I can understand the issue description in detail.
the inspector’s written description shall be displayed.
The system shall display a Measurement table within the Observation tab.
a Measurement table displayed in the Observation tab
I can review measured values related to the observation.
a Measurement Table shall be displayed.
The table shall have columns: Measurement Name, Measured Value, and Result.
I can easily compare recorded readings.
they shall include: Measurement Name, Measured Value, Result.
Result column shall show Pass/Fail as labelled chips or text.
Result shall display Pass/Fail as chips or labeled text.
A Reported Information section shall display below the Measurement table
I can see who reported the observation and when.
a Reported Information section shall appear below it.
Reported Information shall include: Reported By (name), Designation, and Reported On (date & time).
details about who reported the issue
it shall show: Reported By (name), Designation, Reported On (date & time).
The Observation tab shall show narrative fields on the right or bottom: Possible Reason, Risk, Corrective Action, and Remarks.
the Observation tab to include narrative fields
I can review reasoning, risk, actions, and remarks.
the system shall display: Possible Reason, Risk, Corrective Action, Remarks.
Each of these fields shall display multi-line rich text entered by the inspector/engineer.
long and detailed explanations remain readable.
each field shall appear in multi-line rich text.
These narrative fields shall be read-only in this view.
previously recorded reasoning cannot be modified.
On the right side, the system shall display a Media Evidence panel.
a Media Evidence panel on the right side
I can view visual and audio proof for the observation.
a Media Evidence panel shall be shown on the right side.
Media Evidence shall include three sections: Images, Videos, and Audio.
I can easily navigate images, videos, and audio files.
it shall show three sections: Images, Videos, Audio.
The Images section shall show thumbnails of all photos linked to the observation.
image thumbnails shown in the Images section
I can quickly preview all photos linked to the observation.
thumbnails of all linked photos shall be displayed.
The Videos section shall show video thumbnails with a play icon overlay.
video thumbnails to display with a play icon
I can easily recognize and preview video evidence.
the system shall show video thumbnails with a play icon overlay.
The Audio section shall show an inline audio player with play/pause and duration.
an inline audio player for audio files
I can listen to audio evidence directly in the interface.
the system shall show an audio player with play/pause and duration.
Clicking any image/video thumbnail shall open a larger preview (lightbox or full-screen viewer).
open media in a full preview when I click on a thumbnail
I can examine the content in detail.
image or video thumbnails are displayed
a larger preview (lightbox or full-screen viewer) shall open.
Media files shall be view-only; delete or edit actions shall not be available in this screen.
all media files to be view-only
important inspection evidence is not modified or deleted.
the system shall provide no delete or edit options.
When the Testing tab is active, the system shall display a Testing Summary section.
a Testing Summary displayed when I open the Testing tab
I can quickly review testing requirements.
the system shall display a Testing Summary section.
Testing Summary shall show a label “TESTING REQUIRED” followed by a Yes/No indicator chip.
the Testing Summary to show whether testing is required
testing needs are clear at a glance.
it shall show “TESTING REQUIRED” followed by a Yes/No indicator chip.
The system shall display a Timeline tab within the Task Details screen alongside “Observation" and “Testing”.
a Timeline tab in the Task Details screen
I can view the chronological history of the task.
the Task Details screen is loaded
tabs Observation, Testing, and Timeline shall be displayed.
When the user clicks the Timeline tab, the system shall load and display the chronological timeline of the task.
the timeline to load when I click the Timeline tab
I can view the complete task progression.
the user clicks the Timeline tab
the system shall load and display the task’s chronological timeline.
The timeline shall show major task events (e.g., Work Completed, Task Assigned, Action Assigned, Observation Raised by).
the major task events shown in the timeline
I understand the full lifecycle of the task.
the timeline shall show events such as:
Each timeline entry shall display the following: Event Title, Event Date & Time, Performed By (name), Notes/Comments (if available).
each timeline entry to show essential information
I can understand what happened, when, and by whom.
Timeline events shall be ordered from earliest at the top to latest at the bottom OR follow the design’s defined order.
timeline events shown in logical order
I can follow the task flow clearly.
events shall appear earliest at the top, latest at the bottom
—or follow the system’s defined ordering logic.
The system shall show a vertical timeline indicator with dots representing each activity stage.
a vertical timeline indicator with dots
I can visually track progression through task stages.
the system shall show a vertical timeline with dots for each event.
The system shall display a Media Evidence section on the right side containing uploaded media.
a Media Evidence panel displayed on the right side
I can review all supporting media related to the task.
a Media Evidence section shall appear on the right side.

53.title: Show Image Thumbnails Under Media Evidence

content.text: Under Media Evidence → Images, the system shall show all image thumbnails uploaded for the task.
I can quickly preview visual evidence.
the Media Evidence → Images section loads
all image thumbnails shall be displayed.
Under Media Evidence → Videos, the system shall show playable video thumbnails.
I can preview video evidence directly.
Under Media Evidence → Audio, the system shall allow users to play audio files using an integrated audio player.
play audio files within the Media Evidence panel
I can listen to recorded audio evidence.
the system shall allow playback using an inline audio player.
The system shall allow users to click on any media thumbnail to open it in full preview mode.
open any media item in full preview mode
I can view or listen in detail.
the user sees a media thumbnail
the system shall open the media in full preview mode.

57.title: Show Action Buttons at Screen Bottom

content.text: At the bottom of the screen, the system shall show two primary action buttons: Update & Reopen Task and Verify & Close Issue.
two action buttons displayed at the bottom of the screen
I can take the next step in the review process.
the user is on the Task Details screen
the system shall show Update & Reopen Task and Verify & Close Issue buttons.
When the user clicks Update & Reopen Task, the system shall open Reassign task panel on the right side.
the Update & Reopen Task button to open the Reassign Task panel
I can modify and reopen the task.
the user clicks Update & Reopen Task
the Reassign Task panel shall open on the right side.
When the user clicks Verify & Close Issue, the system shall trigger the Close Issue confirmation modal.
clicking Verify & Close Issue to open a confirmation modal
I can confirm the closure of the issue.
the user clicks Verify & Close Issue
the Close Issue confirmation modal shall appear.

60.title: Warning Message in Close Issue Modal

content.text: The modal shall display a warning message: “You’re about to close this issue. The observation will be marked as resolved. Do you want to continue?”
a clear warning message in the confirmation modal
I understand the consequence of closing the issue.
“You’re about to close this issue. The observation will be marked as resolved. Do you want to continue?”
The modal shall contain two buttons: Cancel and Yes, Close Issue.
Cancel and Yes, Close Issue buttons in the modal
I can choose whether to proceed or cancel.
it shall display Cancel and Yes, Close Issue buttons.
Clicking Cancel shall close the modal without performing any action.
clicking Cancel to dismiss the modal
no changes are made to the issue.
the modal shall close with no action performed.
Clicking Yes, Close Issue shall mark the issue as resolved in the database.
the issue to be marked as resolved when I confirm closure
the task officially moves to a completed state.
the user clicks Yes, Close Issue
the issue shall be marked as resolved in the database.
The system shall automatically refresh the Maintenance grid to reflect the updated task status.
the Maintenance grid to refresh automatically after closure
I can immediately see the updated task status.
the system returns to the Maintenance view
the grid shall refresh to reflect the updated status.
After successful closure of issue, the system shall display a toast notification in the top-right corner that reads: “Task closed successfully!”
a success toast after closing an issue
I receive confirmation of successful completion.
a toast shall display: “Task closed successfully!”
The toast shall auto-disappear after 3–5 seconds.
After closure, the Maintenance list shall update the row of the closed issue and change its status to “Completed” or equivalent label.
the Maintenance list to show updated status after closure
completed tasks reflect their new state.
the row shall update its status to Completed or the system’s equivalent label.
The “Action Performed” column shall update to show the final action (e.g., Issue Closed).
the Action Performed column to update after closure
I can see the final recorded action.
the Action Performed column shall show Issue Closed or the relevant final action.
The “Completed On” column shall auto-populate with the closure date and time.
the closure timestamp is accurately recorded.
the Completed-On field shall auto-populate with the current date & time.
When the user clicks Update & Reassign Task, the system shall open the “Reassign Tasks” modal in overlay mode.
Update & Reassign Task to open the Reassign Tasks modal
I can modify task assignment details.
the user clicks Update & Reassign Task
the Reassign Tasks modal shall open in overlay mode.
The modal shall show the title “Reassign Tasks” with a description: “Reassign selected observation tasks to an appropriate maintenance employee.”
the modal to contain a clear title and description
I understand the purpose of reassigning tasks.
Description: “Reassign selected observation tasks to an appropriate maintenance employee.”

72.title: Required Fields in Reassign Tasks Modal

content.text: The modal shall contain the following fields with mandatory indicators: Action Type *, User Assignment *, Priority *, Due Date *and Additional Notes text area shall be displayed at the bottom.
mandatory fields clearly shown in the modal
I provide the required information for reassignment.
include Additional Notes textarea at the bottom.
A close icon (X) at the top-right shall allow the user to close the modal.
an X icon to close the modal
I can exit without submitting changes.
the user clicks the X icon
Two buttons shall be displayed at the bottom: Cancel and Update & Reassign Task.
Cancel and Update & Reassign Task buttons
I can either exit or proceed with updating the task.
The Action Type field shall display a dropdown containing action values such as: Repair, replace, Under Observation,
the Action Type dropdown to list appropriate task actions
I can select the correct action type.
the Action Type dropdown is selected
it shall show: Repair, Replace, Under Observation.
The field is mandatory and shall show validation error if left empty.
validation error when Action Type is empty
I cannot proceed without selecting an action.
the user tries to submit the modal
a validation error shall appear and submission shall be blocked.
User Assignment dropdown shall display a list of all eligible users who can receive the task.
the User Assignment dropdown to show all eligible users
I can assign the task to the correct person.
it shall display a list of all eligible maintenance users.
The system shall allow selecting only one user.
select only one user for task reassignment
the User Assignment dropdown is open
the system shall allow selecting only one user.
When a user is selected, the chip-style UI shall display the selected user (as shown in screenshot).
the selected user to appear as a chip
I can clearly see which user has been chosen for reassignment.
the system shall display the selected user as a chip-style UI element.
Priority dropdown shall provide severity levels such as High, Medium, Low.
the Priority dropdown to show levels such as High, Medium, and Low
I can assign the correct severity for the task.
it shall show: High, Medium, Low.
Changing priority here shall update the task priority in the Maintenance list.
changes to priority in the modal to update the task priority in the Maintenance list
the UI reflects the correct severity after reassignment.
the user updates the task priority
the Maintenance list shall update the task’s priority accordingly.
The Due Date field shall use a date picker component.
the Due Date field to use a date picker
I can easily select a valid date.
the Due Date field is active
a date picker component shall open.
The Additional Notes field shall allow free text input up to a system-defined character limit (e.g., 250 chars).
the Additional Notes field to allow free text with a defined limit
I can provide optional remarks without exceeding system constraints.
the Additional Notes field is displayed
free text input up to 250 characters (or system-defined limit) shall be allowed.
Notes are optional and shall be saved along with the updated task data.
optional notes to be saved with the task
the user enters notes in the Additional Notes field
the notes shall be stored along with the task update.
If mandatory fields are missing, system shall highlight the fields in red and show a validation message.
mandatory fields to highlight in red when they are missing
I know what must be corrected before saving.
The “Update & Reassign Task” button shall remain disabled until required fields are filled.
the Update & Reassign Task button disabled until required fields are filled
the modal loads or user edits fields
the Update & Reassign Task button shall remain disabled.
Clicking Cancel shall close the modal without saving any changes.
clicking Cancel to close the modal without saving
the modal shall close with no changes saved.
Clicking Update & Reassign Task shall trigger a confirmation modal before saving the changes.
a confirmation modal before updating and reassigning
I can confirm the action before changes are applied.
the user clicks Update & Reassign Task
The modal shall display the message: “You’re about to update this issue and reassign it to another reviewer. Do you want to proceed?”
a clear confirmation message before reassignment
I understand the impact of the update.
“You’re about to update this issue and reassign it to another reviewer. Do you want to proceed?”
Modal shall contain two buttons: Cancel and Yes, Update Task.
the confirmation modal to contain Cancel and Yes, Update Task buttons
I can choose to proceed or cancel.
it shall show Cancel and Yes, Update Task.

91.title: Save Updates on Confirmation & Close Modals

content.text: Clicking Cancel shall close the modal without saving changes, and Clicking Yes, Update Task shall save all updates and reassign the task, then After success, the system shall automatically close both modals.
updates saved only when I confirm
no changes occur without explicit approval.
the user clicks Yes, Update Task
After successful update + reassignment, a toast notification shall appear in the top-right corner displaying: “Task Updated & Reassigned Successfully!”
a success toast after updating and reassigning
I know the operation completed successfully.
it does not obstruct my screen.
The Maintenance list shall refresh automatically after task reassignment.
the Maintenance list to refresh after a task is updated and reassigned
I see the latest task information.
the user returns to the Maintenance list
the system shall refresh the grid automatically.
The row corresponding to the reassigned task shall update fields: Assigned To, Priority, Due Date, Status.
the task’s row to reflect new values
updated assignment information is visible immediately.
The updated status chip (e.g., “In-Progress”, “Reassigned”) shall reflect in the UI immediately.
the updated task status chip to appear immediately
I can see whether the task is now “In-Progress” or “Reassigned.”
the system shall display the updated status chip (e.g., In-Progress, Reassigned).
